top_signals.title: Oil Prices Surge 6% Even as Tankers Push Through Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Thin tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued early on Wednesday despite the fresh flare-up in the Middle East, but more vessels appear to be reconsidering moving toward the chokepoint after the U.S. and Iran traded new attacks overnight. At the time of writing, oil prices had spiked 6% on fears of a significant disruption, with Brent futures trading at $78.58 and WTI futures climbing to $74.76. Six tankers were observed to be either completing or starting to move toward the Strait of Hormuz hours after Iran attacked three vessels…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump opposes Carroll getting $5 million damages verdict, cites last-ditch Supreme Court bid
top_signals.summary: Trump has been found liable in two trials for defaming E. Jean Carroll when he denied her claim he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
top_signals.summary: India's central bank called for banks to be barred from crypto exposure as it reiterated support for tighter restrictions on digital assets.

top_signals.title: Canada set to back Teck’s BC smelter to boost germanium output: report

top_signals.title: Supreme Court allows Texas app law requiring age verification to take effect
top_signals.summary: A student advocacy organization and tech trade group had appealed to the high court to stay the Texas App Store Accountability Act on an emergency basis until the lower court rules.
